po_ruling_acknowledged: brand_jordan is a valid explicit brand key for the brand 'Nike Jordan' and is distinct from 'jordan'. No deletion, no merge, no migration.
summary: The display label 'Jordan' is sourced exclusively from Firestore brand_registry/brand_jordan.display_name. All frontend dropdowns render registry display_name verbatim. There is no codebase patch that can change the rendered label. Per dispatch case D, this requires a Firestore write and Homer must STOP and report.

frontend_dropdown_renderers_using_regist: frontend/src/components/admin/UserPortfolioEditor.tsx (uses portfolioBrands array; brand pills derived via registry lookup)
backend_registry_service: backend/functions/src/lib/brandRegistry.ts (loadBrandRegistry → returns brand_key + display_name verbatim from Firestore)
registry_seed_scripts: scripts/tally-product-list-ux-p05-brand-reseed.js — SEED contains 'jordan' but DOES NOT contain 'brand_jordan'
scripts/tally-128-task2-brand-registry-seed.js — references 'Jordan' display in shiekh brand list, no 'brand_jordan' key
scripts/seed/backfill-rics-taxonomy.js — uses substring 'jordan', not the brand_jordan key

anomaly_blocking_code_patch.reason: Display label is data, not code. Source-of-truth is Firestore Document. No source file in the repo currently encodes 'brand_jordan' (or its label). Patching code would be a no-op.
anomaly_blocking_code_patch.implication: Editing brand_registry/brand_jordan.display_name from 'Jordan' → 'Nike Jordan' is the ONLY effective fix. That requires a Firestore write.

alias_collision_risk.concern: brand_registry/jordan currently has aliases=['jordan','brand jordan']. The literal string 'brand jordan' (with space) is an alias under the 'jordan' key. matchBrand() normalizes case+whitespace and would match 'BRAND JORDAN' against the 'jordan' entry, NOT against 'brand_jordan'. This means a CSV import row with raw brand value 'BRAND JORDAN' would be classified as brand_key='jordan' rather than 'brand_jordan'.
alias_collision_risk.evidence_data_point: evidence/tally-product-list-ux-phase5b/413-live-test.txt L36 shows a real import row 'BRAND JORDAN' which under current rules would normalize to brand_key='jordan'.
PO_ruling_needed_followups: Should brand_registry/jordan keep the 'brand jordan' alias, or should that alias migrate to brand_registry/brand_jordan to enforce the distinct business meaning?
Should brand_registry/brand_jordan acquire any aliases? (currently empty array)
If the answer to either is yes, that is a SECOND Firestore write tally and should NOT be folded into the display-label clarification.

proposed_firestore_update_plan_AWAITING_.scope: single field edit on a single document

cons: No audit_log entry tied to a Tally

pros: Reads existing doc, asserts brand_key='brand_jordan' and display_name='Jordan' before write
Writes single field via { merge: true } — does not touch aliases/active/po_confirmed
Emits one summary audit_log entry tied to TALLY-BRAND-JORDAN-REGISTRY-CLARIFICATION
cons: Slightly more setup than a UI edit

proposed_firestore_update_plan_AWAITING_.homer_recommendation: Option 2 (script) — matches established Tally evidence-trail pattern, leaves an audit_log breadcrumb, dry-run protected. Same pattern as TALLY-D3-E-CADENCE-RESIDUE.
